name: Set-PSDebug

options.name: -Off
options.description: Turns off all script debugging features.

options.name: -Step
options.description: Turns on script stepping. Before each line runs, PowerShell prompts you to stop, continue, or enter a new interpreter level to inspect the state of the script.

options.name: -Strict
options.description: Specifies that variables must be assigned a value before being referenced in a script. If a variable is referenced before a value is assigned, PowerShell returns an exception error. This is equivalent to `Set-StrictMode -Version 1`. For more information, see Set-StrictMode (Set-StrictMode.md).

options.name: -Trace
options.description: Specifies the trace level for each line in a script. Each line is traced as it's run.

description: Turns script debugging features on and off, sets the trace level, and toggles strict mode.
